violations.finding: Chinese text present in env block: "_comment_anthropic": "以下为可选配置（已禁用）"
violations.recommendation: Translate the value to English, e.g. "The following is optional config (disabled)". The settings.json schema does not require this key; if it is purely an internal comment marker, consider deleting it instead.
violations.evidence: git blame settings.json line 22 → commit 2c87cbaf (Yugoge, 2026-05-17). The dev-20260519-161035 cycle diff hunks for settings.json touch lines ~173-185, ~297, ~318-330, ~360-370, ~754 — line 22 was NOT modified by this cycle. Reported per Standard 6 unconditional-scan exception (agents/style-inspector.md Standard 6). Per close.md AC-2.6(b) line-level diff-overlap + pre-diff-baseline rule, this is a pre-existing baseline finding, classified as ADVISORY (not a blocker for this cycle's closeness gate).

standards_notes.no-inline-code: Scope contains no command/agent .md files. The convention.md is pure prose under docs/reference/ (no executable code blocks). PASS.
standards_notes.no-hardcoded-values: tmp-cleanup.sh contains literal paths /tmp, /var/tmp/codex-outputs, /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log, /tmp/claude-0 — these are the script's PURPOSE (it is the daemon that sweeps these specific paths under the /etc/cron.d/tmp-cleanup-daily contract). Documented in the file header (lines 1-33). Parameterizing them would invalidate the contract. Not violations. THRESHOLD=75 / RATE_LIMIT=3 in the new hooks are spec-required constants for the dev-20260519-161035 plan, documented in header. PASS.
standards_notes.use-source-venv: userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:39 uses `python3 -c` for a 9-line inline JSON-parsing one-liner with stdlib-only imports (json, os, sys). This is the established codebase idiom — same pattern is used in pretool-block-production.sh:8/17, pretool-block-production-files.sh:8/16/67/130, pretool-write-guard.sh:10/18/22/50, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:9, userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:18/26/29/51/122/140, prehook-overnight-worktree-check.sh:12/25, pretool-block-enterworktree.sh:17/27, session-promote-hook.sh:35, push.sh:86/88/248/92. Standard 3 targets Python SCRIPT invocations (real .py files), not stdlib-only inline `python3 -c` one-liners. Flagging here would create inconsistency with the entire hooks/ directory. PASS.
standards_notes.integer-step-numbering: No step numbering in any of the 5 files. N/A → PASS.
standards_notes.meaningful-naming: session-tmpfs-banner.sh and userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh names describe their purpose (the SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit lifecycles, the tmpfs subject matter, the banner-vs-pressure roles). No enhance-/fast-/v2 patterns. PASS.
standards_notes.english-only: ONE finding at settings.json:22 (pre-existing baseline, see violations[]). Unconditional scan against agents/*.md, commands/*.md, .claude/agents/*.md, .claude/commands/*.md performed: hits at commands/close.md:374-388 and commands/dev.md:253/357/606/655/795/1475/1477 — all OUTSIDE this cycle's changed-files scope and pre-existing per git history; not reported per scoping contract. PASS for newly introduced content.
standards_notes.merge-similar-scripts: session-tmpfs-banner.sh and userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh occupy distinct hook lifecycle slots (SessionStart vs UserPromptSubmit) and emit different outputs (one-shot banner vs rate-limited pressure warning). Not merge candidates. tmp-cleanup.sh is a new daemon script with no overlap with existing /tmp sweepers. PASS.
standards_notes.concise-documentation: convention.md is 23 lines, tightly scoped, no marketing language, no repetitive examples, no pseudocode. PASS.
standards_notes.parameterized-scripts: All scripts in scope either accept appropriate parameters (tmp-cleanup.sh accepts --dry-run as positional $1, default=delete-mode) or hold spec-required constants documented in their headers (THRESHOLD=75, RATE_LIMIT=3). PASS.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: PreToolUse:Skill hook error: [python3 "$HOME/.claude/hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py"]: BLOCKED by tool-policy.v1: {"role":"style-inspector","tool":"Skill","target":null,"deny_reason":"tool Skill not in allowed_tools"}. Codex consultation could not be performed because the style-inspector role's tool policy denies the Skill tool. Self-review substituted per agents/style-inspector.md graceful-fallback protocol.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted; 6 adversarial questions answered against the draft verdict — (1) false-positive sweep for THRESHOLD/RATE_LIMIT constants concluded NOT a violation (spec-required, header-documented); (2) false-positive sweep for `python3 -c` inline one-liner concluded NOT a violation (consistent with 10+ sibling-hook idioms in the same repo); (3) hardcoded paths in tmp-cleanup.sh concluded NOT a violation (script purpose, header-documented contract with /etc/cron.d/tmp-cleanup-daily); (4) missed-violation sweep for Standard 1 N/A (no command/agent .md files in scope); (5) severity calibration for settings.json:22 confirmed advisory per close.md AC-2.6(b) line-level rule; (6) dangling-references S10 all targets confirmed to exist on disk via `ls -la` checks. No changes to draft verdict.
